response.justification: Bogdan is an experienced polyglot engineer with strong software craftsmanship practices and a broad full-stack background, but his primary professional stack for the past decade is TypeScript and Node.js, not Java or Kotlin. Java appears briefly in the skills list and is mentioned peripherally in two roles (robotic arms at Altom, NI LabWindows), but there is no evidence of deep JVM expertise, Spring ecosystem experience, enterprise framework knowledge (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate), build tool proficiency (Maven/Gradle), or IDE internals work at the depth this role requires. The core technical mismatch is too substantial for a position explicitly requiring strong Java/Kotlin JVM specialization.
strengths: Consistent software craftsmanship discipline across roles: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, and trunk-based development
Relevant developer-tooling exposure: contributed to NI LabWindows (a C IDE) and currently builds MCP code generators and agentic developer tooling at re:cinq
Demonstrated ability to own complex systems end-to-end across multiple languages and domains, including low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin depth: the language appears only as a brief mention in two early roles; the last decade of professional work is TypeScript/Node.js-first
Zero Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience evident anywhere in the resume or skills section
No JVM internals knowledge, enterprise build tool expertise (Maven/Gradle), or application server integration work documented

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
